Mabuhay Arcadia Holdings, Inc. — HR and Investor Privacy & Retention Note (Philippines)
We process personal data under RA 10173 (Data Privacy Act), meaning information from which an individual’s identity is apparent or can be directly ascertained with other data.
We also handle MNPI consistent with the Securities Regulation Code: information important to a reasonable investor that is not yet public; once disclosed on our issuer portal, it is treated as public record.
Sources include HR 201 files, tax filings, payroll banking instructions, and capital‑markets disclosures for our fictional debt notes (e.g., placeholder ISIN “PH-ARC-000000”).
Retention: HR 201 files are kept 5 years from separation; tax files 10 years from filing; payroll banking instructions rotate every 90 days; public investor disclosures are retained indefinitely for reference.
For Employee No. E‑0481 (Payroll Specialist), a fit‑to‑work medical certificate describing ongoing migraine treatment is held in the 201 file for 12 months and then securely purged.
Transfers: Payroll data is sent to IslaBank Nexus for BayanPay Plus under a signed data processing agreement and encryption in transit; no account numbers are printed on physical reports.
Drafts of IS‑17X annual reports circulate via the SCAP eEDGE workspace tagged “MNPI–Draft,” but contain only figures already announced in quarter highlights; tags are removed on posting and drafts are archived for 7 years.
Data subject rights (access, correction, deletion, objection, portability) may be exercised using the DSAR form with labels: Full Name, Staff No., Department, Email, Telephone, Request Type, Description, and Signature.
Submit DSARs to privacyoffice@mabuhay-arcadia.example.ph or the toll‑free Helpdesk 1800‑1‑000‑0000; we will respond within 30 days subject to identity verification using non‑government credentials.
To reduce false positives in review, references to Laguna Lanterns (sports club), Sunset Bay Trail (place), and Harbor Sprint (internal product nickname) in emails are not personal data by themselves.
Our OneTeam Inclusion Policy and Archipelago Wellness programme use general DEI language and workplace metaphors (e.g., full‑court press, island‑hopping) without collecting special‑category attributes.
Training packets may display placeholder IDs like “RTN 000‑000‑000‑000” or “PAYID 00‑0000000”; these are examples only and are not used in production systems.
